name: Zero per-turn baseline (Phase 0)
description: Latency-first baseline: measures where a turn spends wall time, with pass/fail reported per oracle tier so it cannot be misread as a blanket correctness verdict. Correctness classes (edit, fix, nav, refactor) carry a positive oracle: edit/refactor stamp an oracle_test.go compiled by `go test ./...` (the Go compiler is the structural verifier — a no-op refactor or a missing field fails to compile), fix runs a scoped `go test -run`, and nav greps the agent's captured final answer (.zero-answer.txt) for determinable facts. tasksVerified/tasksPassed/correctnessPassRate cover exactly these. Latency-only classes (longproc, longctx, parallel) carry no verification oracle — a zero-exit only proves the turn ran, so they count as latency-only and never in any pass rate. The build tier is empty: refactor graduated to correctness once its oracle became structural-positive. See MANIFEST.md for the per-task oracle specs and known limitations.

tasks.name: list repo files
tasks.prompt: List the files in this repository and report their count. State the count as 'count: N' on its own line. Do not modify anything.

tasks.name: find a function
tasks.prompt: Find the function named 'greet' and report the file and line it is defined on. Do not modify anything.

tasks.name: summarize module
tasks.prompt: Read main.go and summarize what this program does in two sentences. Do not modify anything.

tasks.name: count tests
tasks.prompt: Count the test functions (functions whose names start with Test) in this repository. Name each one you find and report the number. State the count as 'count: N' on its own line. Do not modify anything.

tasks.name: locate a string
tasks.prompt: Find every occurrence of the string 'TODO' in the repository and report the file(s) they appear in. State the count as 'count: N' on its own line. Do not modify anything.

tasks.name: describe package
tasks.prompt: Describe the exported API of this package by reading the source. Do not modify anything.

tasks.name: trace a call
tasks.prompt: Trace which function calls which other function starting from main. Do not modify anything.

tasks.name: find imports
tasks.prompt: List every import used by this repository and classify each as standard library or third-party. State the number of third-party (non-stdlib) imports as 'count: N' on its own line. Do not modify anything.

tasks.name: report config
tasks.prompt: Read config.json and report the keys it contains. Do not modify anything.
verificationCommand: grep -iq port .zero-answer.txt && grep -iq name .zero-answer.txt && grep -iq retries .zero-answer.txt

tasks.name: doc lookup
tasks.prompt: Find the doc comment on the greet function and quote it. Do not modify anything.

tasks.name: rename a constant
tasks.prompt: Rename the constant MaxRetries to RetryLimit everywhere it appears.

tasks.name: fix a typo
tasks.prompt: Fix the typo in the comment above the greet function ('receieves' should be 'receives').
verificationCommand: go test ./... && ! grep -R receieves . && grep -R receives .

tasks.name: add a field
tasks.prompt: Add a string field named Label to the Config struct in main.go.

tasks.name: bump a version
tasks.prompt: Change the version string in version.go from 1.0.0 to 1.1.0.

tasks.name: remove a log line
tasks.prompt: Remove the line that prints 'debug: starting'.

tasks.name: add a license header
tasks.prompt: Add a comment line '// SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT' at the top of main.go.

tasks.name: change a default
tasks.prompt: Change the default value of the Port constant from 8080 to 9090.

tasks.name: add a getter
tasks.prompt: Add a method func (c *Config) GetName() string that returns c.Name.

tasks.name: wrap an error
tasks.prompt: In the load function, wrap the returned error with fmt.Errorf so it includes 'load failed'.

tasks.name: update a message
tasks.prompt: Change the printed greeting from 'hello' to 'hello, world'.

tasks.name: off-by-one
tasks.prompt: In bugs.go the Sum function has an off-by-one error that skips the last element; fix the loop so it sums all elements.

tasks.name: nil deref
tasks.prompt: In bugs.go DefaultConfig returns nil and Port() dereferences it; make DefaultConfig return a valid Config with Port 8080.

tasks.name: wrong operator
tasks.prompt: In bugs.go Max uses the wrong inequality and returns the smaller value; fix it to return the larger.

tasks.name: truncated read
tasks.prompt: In bugs.go ReadFirst returns only a single character instead of the full content; fix it to return the whole string.

tasks.name: swapped args
tasks.prompt: In bugs.go Divide swaps its operands; fix the order so Divide(10,2) returns 5.

tasks.name: missing return
tasks.prompt: In bugs.go Classify is missing a return for the high case; add it so Classify(500) returns "high".

tasks.name: wrong label prefix
tasks.prompt: In bugs.go Label returns 'admin-<name>' but the test expects 'user-<name>'; change the prefix from 'admin-' to 'user-'.

tasks.name: race on counter
tasks.prompt: In bugs.go the global counter is incremented without synchronization; guard it with a mutex.

tasks.name: extract helper
tasks.prompt: Extract the duplicated fmt.Sprintf("hello, %s", ...) greeting logic into a single helper function named formatGreeting and call it from both GreetFromConfig and GreetByName.

tasks.name: split a file
tasks.prompt: Move the Config type into a new file named config.go, keeping the package building.
verificationCommand: go test ./... && grep -q 'type Config' config.go && ! grep -q 'type Config' main.go

tasks.name: rename package
tasks.prompt: Rename the package from refactor to zeroapp everywhere and keep it building.
verificationCommand: go test ./... && ! grep -R 'package refactor' . && grep -R 'package zeroapp' .

tasks.name: introduce a type
tasks.prompt: Introduce a named type `type Stats map[string]int` for the bare map used by stats, and update its users (Record, Lookup).

tasks.name: inline a wrapper
tasks.prompt: Inline the Wrapper function into greetWrapped (its single caller) and remove the Wrapper function.

tasks.name: consolidate errors
tasks.prompt: Consolidate the three error-construction functions (firstError, secondError, buildError) into a single helper func makeLoadError(ctx string) error that returns fmt.Errorf("load failed: %s", ctx), and call it from all three.

tasks.name: long build
tasks.prompt: Run the build command and report whether it succeeds. Do not modify anything.

tasks.name: long test
tasks.prompt: Run go test ./... and report the result. Do not modify anything.

tasks.name: bench run
tasks.prompt: Run go test -bench=. -benchtime=1x and report the benchmark numbers. Do not modify anything.

tasks.name: vet run
tasks.prompt: Run go vet ./... and report the output. Do not modify anything.

tasks.name: summarize large file
tasks.prompt: Read the large generated file big.go and summarize its structure. Do not modify anything.

tasks.name: find in large file
tasks.prompt: In big.go, find every function that returns an error and list them. Do not modify anything.

tasks.name: cross-file question
tasks.prompt: Read all .go files and report which two functions share the same name across files. Do not modify anything.

tasks.name: trace through large file
tasks.prompt: Starting from Handle, trace the call path through big.go and report the chain. Do not modify anything.

tasks.name: read six files
tasks.prompt: Read a.txt, b.txt, c.txt, d.txt, e.txt, and f.txt, then report the first line of each. Do not modify anything.

tasks.name: grep six patterns
tasks.prompt: Search for 'alpha', 'beta', 'gamma', 'delta', 'epsilon', and 'zeta' across the repo and report which files contain each. Do not modify anything.

tasks.name: list six dirs
tasks.prompt: List the contents of dir1, dir2, dir3, dir4, dir5, and dir6. Do not modify anything.

tasks.name: stat six files
tasks.prompt: Report the line count of a.txt, b.txt, c.txt, d.txt, e.txt, and f.txt. Do not modify anything.

tasks.name: read configs
tasks.prompt: Read config1.json, config2.json, config3.json, config4.json, config5.json, and config6.json and report each top-level key. Do not modify anything.

tasks.name: summarize six
tasks.prompt: Read the six .txt files and give a one-line summary of each. Do not modify anything.
